prompt: Create a realistic shallow-depth-of-field tabletop photo of a handmade layered paper-cut shadowbox figurine of a fantasy alchemist girl standing on a green self-healing cutting mat in a craft workshop. The figure is built from many stacked cut cardstock layers with visible brown cardboard edges, like a 3D paper model or failed papercraft pattern test, and has a deliberately imperfect construction. She wears a pale mint-green long coat with layered skirt panels, tan leather straps crossing her chest and waist, brown boots and gloves, and a belt full of small potion bottles. Count 7 visible potion bottles: 1 purple bottle raised in her left hand, 1 small bottle on her left hip pouch, 3 bottles hanging across the front belt, and 2 bottles on the right side of the skirt. Her hair is pale icy blue, long, wavy, voluminous, and cut into separate stacked paper layers; her face is intentionally hidden by a centered square pixelated beige blur censor block. Pose her dynamically as if skipping or floating, one arm raised holding the bottle, the other hand open to the side, one boot lifted, attached to a small layered oval display base with tiny paper grass and plants. Surround the figurine with exactly 5 main crafting items: 1 white squeeze bottle of craft glue on the left with a cute bear label reading Bearly Art Precision Craft Glue, 1 metal ruler on the right, 1 silver hobby knife at the lower right, 1 green cutting mat with white grid lines underneath, and multiple brown cardboard offcuts and flat pattern pieces in the background. Use warm natural indoor light, macro lens perspective, realistic paper texture, soft bokeh background, muted earthy colors, and a high-detail photorealistic craft-desk aesthetic.
